contextBlurb: Quebec's housing policy is administered through the Société d'habitation du Québec (SHQ) and the Tribunal administratif du logement (TAL). Key initiatives include the Programme d'habitation abordable Québec (PHAQ) and Bill 20, an Act to facilitate access to housing. The province regulates landlord-tenant relations, subsidized housing eligibility, and affordable housing construction through legislation and programs.

legislation.title: An Act to enact the Act to facilitate access to housing and to amend the Act respecting the Société d'habitation du Québec
legislation.summary: Amends the Act respecting the Société d'habitation du Québec to allow loan guarantees, prescribe compensation for lessees who cease to be eligible for affordable housing, and expand the scope of low-rental housing to affordable housing.

legislation.title: Initiative de multilogements hautement préfabriqués – Programme d'habitation abordable Québec
legislation.summary: A component of the Programme d'habitation abordable Québec that partners housing organizations with a design-builder selected by the SHQ to develop highly prefabricated multi-unit residential projects.

legislation.title: Programme de renouvellement des actifs en culture et infrastructures municipales – Volet 1 projets de bâtiments de base sans mise en commun
legislation.summary: A 2025–2028 infrastructure program guiding the construction of basic building projects without shared facilities, applicable to single-beneficiary municipal infrastructure projects.

legislation.title: Reconnaissance d'un propriétaire de logements étudiants
legislation.summary: Establishes a recognition process for owners of student housing buildings, requiring that after five years more than half of the units must be rented to students, with owners submitting applications to the minister.

legislation.title: Droits et obligations des locateurs et des locataires
legislation.summary: Establishes the rules governing landlord and tenant obligations regarding access to housing, property visits, noise disturbances, and dispute resolution procedures in Quebec.

legislation.title: Subsidized Housing – Québec Rent Supplement Program
legislation.summary: Allows low-income households to pay rent equal to 25% of their income through housing units in the private rental market or owned by cooperatives and non-profit organizations.

legislation.title: Échanges entre le Ministère et le Tribunal administratif du logement relatifs aux évictions
legislation.summary: Proposed reforms discussed between the Ministry and the Tribunal administratif du logement (TAL) to allow punitive damages against landlords in cases of insalubrity and insecurity, and to anonymize tenant applications.

legislation.title: Analyse d'impact réglementaire – Projet de règlement modifiant les critères du ministère des Affaires municipales et de l'Habitation
legislation.summary: The Ministère des Affaires municipales et de l'Habitation applies the Government Policy on Regulatory Streamlining to its regulatory process as outlined in the Strategic Plan 2023–2027, including amendments to housing-related regulations.

legislation.title: Subsidized Housing Program (Low-Rental Housing / HLM)
